question: Write a C++ program to concatenate two one-dimensional integer arrays into a third array. The program should take the sizes and elements of the first two arrays from the user, then populate and print the concatenated array.

test_cases.expected:
Enter the size of the first array: Enter 3 elements for the first array:
Element 1: Element 2: Element 3: Enter the size of the second array: Enter 2 elements for the second array:
Element 1: Element 2: First array: 1 2 3
Second array: 4 5
Concatenated array: 1 2 3 4 5

Enter the size of the first array: Enter 0 elements for the first array:
Enter the size of the second array: Enter 0 elements for the second array:
First array: (empty)
Second array: (empty)
Concatenated array: (empty)

Enter the size of the first array: Enter 1 elements for the first array:
Element 1: Enter the size of the second array: Enter 0 elements for the second array:
First array: 100
Second array: (empty)
Concatenated array: 100

Enter the size of the first array: Enter 2 elements for the first array:
Element 1: Element 2: Enter the size of the second array: Array size cannot be negative. Please enter a non-negative integer.
Enter 3 elements for the second array:
Element 1: Element 2: Element 3: First array: 10 20
Second array: 30 40 50
Concatenated array: 10 20 30 40 50

Enter the size of the first array: Enter 2 elements for the first array:
Element 1: Element 2: Invalid input. Please enter an integer.
Element 2: Enter the size of the second array: Enter 1 elements for the second array:
Element 1: First array: 10 20
Second array: 30
Concatenated array: 10 20 30
